item.message: Skill descriptions were shortened to fit the 2% skills context budget. Codex can still see every skill, but some descriptions are shorter. Disable unused skills or plugins to leave more room for the rest.

item.text: I’ll inspect the three source modules, then make the smallest compatible schema/model/query changes and run the test suite.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/queries.mjs && git status --short"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,240p' src/schema.mjs && sed -n '1,240p' src/accounts.mjs && sed -n '1,260p' src/queries.mjs && git status --short"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' package.json && rg -n \"accountId|tier|CREATE TABLE accounts|accounts\" README.md tests 2>/dev/null || true"
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' package.json && rg -n \"accountId|tier|CREATE TABLE accounts|accounts\" README.md tests 2>/dev/null || true"

item.text: The accounts table stores entity fields in its JSONB document, so I’m adding an optional database constraint for the allowed values and validating writes in the account model; existing documents remain valid because the field is optional.

item.text: The first test exposed that the generated enterprise `accounts` table does not expose a `doc` column at schema-initialization time, so I’m checking its actual columns and will place the constraint on the real JSONB field rather than assuming its name.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "node --input-type=module -e 'import { withDb } from \"./src/db.mjs\"; await withDb(async (client) => { const r = await client.query(\"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema = current_schema() AND table_name = "'$1 ORDER BY ordinal_position", ["accounts"]); console.log(r.rows); });'"'"

/bin/zsh -lc "node --input-type=module -e 'import { withDb } from \"./src/db.mjs\"; await withDb(async (client) => { const r = await client.query(\"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema = current_schema() AND table_name = "'$1 ORDER BY ordinal_position", ["accounts"]); console.log(r.rows); });'"'"

/bin/zsh -lc 'npm test && git diff --check && git diff -- src/schema.mjs src/accounts.mjs src/queries.mjs'

item.text:
Implemented `preferredPaymentMethod` support across accounts and order summaries.

- Optional enum validation and database constraint added.
- Account create/update persists the field.
- `getOrderSummary` now returns `preferredPaymentMethod`.
- Aligned the generated database schema with the existing JSONB-based account/order/invoice code paths.

Verified with `npm test` — acceptance suite passes.
